 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.1","title":"BrowserAutomation scaffolding (Playwright init, profiles dir, safe logging)","description":"# Task: BrowserAutomation scaffolding

## Goal
Create the browser automation layer with correct lifecycle handling.

## Requirements
- Lazy Playwright initialization.
- Profiles directory under wa data dir:
  - `~/.local/share/wa/browser_profiles/<service>/<account>/`
- Headless vs non-headless modes:
  - default to non-headless during early development
  - headless possible once stable
- Safe logging:
  - never log device codes or secrets

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - profile path resolution is workspace/config correct
- Integration tests:
  - can launch a browser context and open a local page fixture

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can launch a browser context and open a page.
- Profile directories are deterministic and workspace-scoped.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.2","title":"Implement OpenAI/Codex device auth flow (auth.openai.com/codex/device)","description":"# Task: OpenAI device auth flow (Playwright)

## Goal
Automate the OpenAI device-code flow used by Codex CLI via Playwright in a way that is:
- robust to small UI changes
- safe with respect to secrets (no password storage)
- debuggable when it fails (screenshots/HTML dumps, redacted logs)

Target URL:
- `https://auth.openai.com/codex/device`

## Preconditions / inputs
- A persistent Playwright profile exists for the target account (profile dir derived from workspace/config).
- The workflow step has already obtained a `user_code` (device code) from the Codex pane.

## Happy path (v0)
1. Navigate to the device page.
2. Ensure we are in a logged-in state for the intended account:
   - if already logged in: proceed
   - if email prompt appears: fill email, continue
   - if password/MFA required: **do not attempt to automate**; exit into the fallback path (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
3. Fill the `user_code` form field.
4. Submit.
5. Verify success via a stable marker:
   - preferred: page contains “Successfully logged in” (or equivalent stable selector)
   - also capture URL + final DOM snapshot for debugging

## Failure modes
- Password/MFA prompts:
  - return a structured error that the workflow can treat as “interactive bootstrap required”
  - point to the fallback task (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
- Rate limit / bot detection / unexpected page:
  - fail with actionable error and attach redacted artifacts

## Logging / artifacts
- Never log passwords, session cookies, access tokens, or raw HTML that may contain secrets.
- On failure, write artifacts under the workspace artifacts dir:
  - screenshot
  - redacted DOM snapshot (or a hash + minimal selector debug)
  - a short, human-readable failure report

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - parse + validate the `user_code` format before browser automation (fail fast)
- Integration tests (offline):
  - run Playwright against a local HTML fixture that simulates the device page, verifying selector strategy
- Manual smoke tests:
  - a `wa browser smoke-test --openai-device` (tracked elsewhere) confirms it can complete on a real profile

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a valid persistent profile that is already authenticated, this flow completes and returns “success” for a real account.
- If password/MFA is required, the flow exits safely with a clear “interactive bootstrap required” error and produces redacted artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.3","title":"Profile persistence + MFA/password fallback (one-time interactive bootstrap)","description":"# Task: Profile persistence + MFA fallback

## Goal
Make auth reliable in the real world where MFA exists.

## Strategy
- Prefer persistent profiles so most auth runs require no credential entry.
- If Playwright detects password/MFA prompts:
  - open interactive browser window
  - prompt the operator to complete login once
  - then persist storage state for future automated runs

## Safety
- Never store passwords.
- Never log raw MFA codes, cookies, or OAuth redirect URLs with sensitive params.

## Testing
- Manual test plan:
  - first run requires interactive bootstrap
  - subsequent runs succeed without password prompts

## Acceptance Criteria
- After one interactive bootstrap, subsequent runs succeed without password prompts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.4","title":"Browser smoke-test command (manual) + failure reporting conventions","description":"# Task: Browser smoke-test command (manual)

## Goal
Provide a simple, human-invokable command to validate Playwright integration **outside** full workflows.

When selectors change (which they will), we want a fast feedback loop:
- run one command
- get a precise failure
- fix the automation

## Proposed CLI surface
- `wa auth test openai --account <name>`
  - exercises the OpenAI/Codex device auth flow end-to-end

- Optional flags:
  - `--profile <name>` (select persistent browser profile)
  - `--timeout-secs <n>`
  - `--headful` (default headless; headful for debugging)

## Output & error conventions
- Always return a structured outcome **matching the auth realities matrix** (`wa-nu4.1.4.5`):
  - `Success` → maps to `Automated`
  - `NeedsHuman` → maps to `NeedsHuman`
  - `Failed` → maps to `Fail`

- Failure output must include:
  - which step failed
  - current page URL (redacted)
  - recommended next action (update selector, run headful, etc.)

## Safety requirements
- Never ask for or store passwords.
- Redact URLs containing tokens/codes in logs.
- Always close contexts/pages on all code paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running the command either succeeds or returns a precise error with next steps.
- Outcome categories are consistent with the auth realities matrix (no new ad-hoc states).
- Errors are stable and actionable (not vague “something failed”).


## Testing
- Manual test plan:
  - Run headless + headful modes.
  - Validate all three outcomes are reachable in controlled conditions:
    - Success (already-authenticated profile)
    - NeedsHuman (profile not authenticated / MFA)
    - Failed (simulate selector mismatch)

- Redaction tests:
  - Ensure logs/artifacts do not contain device codes or tokenized URLs.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.5","title":"Auth realities matrix — OpenAI/Codex","description":"# Task: Auth realities matrix — OpenAI/Codex

## Goal
Establish a **factual, repeatable** understanding of which OpenAI/Codex auth paths can be fully automated and which require a one-time human bootstrap.

This resolves PLAN.md Open Question #3 for the OpenAI/Codex path and prevents workflows from guessing.

## Why this matters (user value)
When a pane hits usage limits, wa must decide whether to:
- proceed automatically, or
- pause and request a human action

A documented matrix avoids brittle heuristics and makes failures explainable.

## Scope
- OpenAI/Codex device auth flow only (Phase 2).
- Manual exploration of **real-world states** and how to detect them:
  - already authenticated profile
  - needs device code entry
  - MFA/password required
  - SSO/enterprise redirect
  - rate-limited / captcha
- Outcome taxonomy to standardize across automation:
  - `Automated`
  - `NeedsHuman`
  - `Fail`

## Deliverables
1. **Auth realities matrix** (checked into docs or spec notes):
   - state name
   - detection signals (selectors / URLs / CLI prompts)
   - automation outcome (`Automated`, `NeedsHuman`, `Fail`)
   - required human steps (if any)
   - safe retry guidance

2. **Outcome taxonomy + workflow guidance**:
   - how `handle_usage_limits` should message `NeedsHuman`
   - how PolicyEngine should respond when auth is not automatable

3. **Redaction rules**:
   - define what must be scrubbed in auth artifacts (device codes, tokenized URLs)

> Note: Smoke-test alignment against this matrix is tracked in `wa-nu4.1.4.4`.

## Testing / validation (manual)
- Run manual auth checks in controlled scenarios:
  - already-authenticated profile → `Automated`
  - fresh profile → `NeedsHuman`
  - forced MFA/SSO (if available) → `NeedsHuman`

- Redaction checks:
  - notes/artifacts must not contain device codes or auth tokens

## Acceptance Criteria
- A documented matrix exists for **all OpenAI/Codex auth states** observed during testing.
- The outcome taxonomy is explicit and referenced by workflow fallback text.
- Redaction rules are written and unambiguous.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.5","title":"[EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth)","description":"# Accounts + usage integration

## Goal
Integrate `caut` (coding_agent_usage_tracker) so wa can make quota-aware decisions.

## Core idea
`caut` is the usage truth; wa mirrors data for history and selection.

## Deliverables
- wrappers around `caut usage` and `caut refresh` (JSON)
- selection policy: highest percent remaining + LRU tie-break
- persistence to accounts table



## Success Criteria
- `caut` integration provides typed usage/account data and maps errors into actionable UX.
- Account selection is deterministic (threshold + LRU tie-break) and explainable.
- DB mirror supports listing/previewing selection decisions in robot/human surfaces.
- Unit/integration tests cover parsing, selection determinism, and DB updates.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - JSON parsing fixtures for `caut usage` and `caut refresh` outputs (including partial/malformed outputs and stderr noise).
  - Selection determinism tests (tie-breakers, thresholds, LRU updates).

- Integration tests:
  - Use a fake/fixture `caut` implementation to exercise the wrapper without hitting real accounts.
  - Verify DB mirror updates and robot/human views never expose secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.5.1","title":"Implement caut wrapper (usage/refresh JSON parsing, error mapping)","description":"# Task: caut wrapper (usage/refresh)

## Goal
Treat `caut` as the authoritative usage/account source and expose a small internal API that:
- invokes `caut` safely (timeouts, error capture)
- parses its JSON output into typed Rust structs
- maps failures into actionable, user-friendly errors

## Why
wa should not re-implement remote API logic that already exists in `caut`. The wrapper is the “stability layer” between:
- `wa` workflows/robots that need usage data
- the external `caut` CLI which can drift slightly in output format

## Scope
- Supported subcommands (v0):
  - `caut usage --service <svc> --format json`
  - `caut refresh --service <svc> --format json`
- Supported services (initial): `openai` (extend later)

## Design
- Provide a wrapper module that:
  - builds the command line
  - executes with a hard timeout
  - captures stdout/stderr
  - limits output size
  - parses JSON with `serde`
- Error mapping:
  - `caut` missing: actionable install hint
  - non-zero exit: include stderr summary (bounded)
  - invalid JSON: include a small redacted preview + version info if available

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - parse known-good fixtures
  - parse edge-case fixtures (missing fields, new fields, commas in numbers)
  - validate that unknown fields do not break parsing if possible
  - error mapping behavior (missing binary, timeout, invalid json)
- Integration tests:
  - a small “fake caut” test binary/script in tests (or dependency injection) to simulate exit codes and stderr

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a sample `caut` JSON fixture (usage + refresh), parsing succeeds and produces a stable typed representation.
- Failures (missing binary, timeout, invalid JSON) produce actionable errors without panics and without logging secrets.

All 5 unit tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-25T16:03:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.7","title":"Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets)","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle tests

## Goal
Ensure diagnostic bundles are reliable and safe.

## Testing
- Bundle layout contains required files.
- Redaction applied (no secrets in outputs).
- Stable metadata present (`version`, `enabled_features`).
- Reservation snapshot present when reservations exist (owner/TTL, redacted).
- If bundle already exists and `--force` not set, command fails safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a running WezTerm instance (use fixture DB and fixture config).


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","title":"Doctor tests: fixture-based checks + output stability (JSON/TTY)","description":"# Task: `wa doctor` tests

## Goal
Make `wa doctor` a **trustworthy** and **regression-resistant** diagnostic surface.

`wa doctor` is how users decide whether to trust the tool. If it flakes, lies, or leaks secrets, we lose users.

## Test scope

### Unit tests
- Path resolution rendering (workspace root, DB/log/lock paths) is correct and deterministic.
- JSON output shape is stable (fields present, naming stable, no secret leakage).
- Redaction is applied to any potentially sensitive field (even on error paths).

### Integration tests (fixture-driven; no real WezTerm required)
Run `wa doctor` in a hermetic test environment that simulates:
- A healthy system.
- Missing `wezterm` binary / `wezterm cli list` failure.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB unreadable / schema mismatch.
- Watcher not running / lock held / stale lock.
- Optional feature checks present/absent (mcp/browser/web/tui) without forcing those deps.

## Test strategy / harness design
- Prefer dependency injection where feasible (traits) so we can simulate:
  - `wezterm` CLI calls
  - storage open/migration version
  - watcher health snapshot
- If process-based stubbing is simpler, use a test harness that:
  - writes a small “fake wezterm” executable to a temp dir
  - prepends that temp dir to `PATH`
  - feeds deterministic stdout/stderr for specific subcommands

## Output stability requirements
- Non-TTY mode:
  - `wa doctor --format json` must be strictly machine-parseable and stable.
  - Tests validate JSON against a versioned schema (if/when we add a schema for doctor output).
- TTY mode:
  - Tests validate key content (not exact ANSI art); either strip ANSI or snapshot a normalized rendering.

## Logging & debuggability
- Tests must capture logs on failure and print them (bounded) to make failures diagnosable.
- Ensure logs do not leak secrets (redaction applied to any dynamic content).

## Deliverables
- Unit test module(s) for doctor output + redaction.
- Integration test harness utilities for stubbing wezterm/storage/watcher.
- A small set of golden fixtures covering the major failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa doctor` has coverage for each major check and for each major failure mode.
- Tests do not require:
  - network access
  - a real WezTerm instance
  - a real user home directory
- Adding a new doctor check requires adding at least one test covering both success and failure paths.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the integration harness actually uses the fake `wezterm` binary (assert invocation).
  - Add an explicit assertion that `--format json` output contains no ANSI escapes and validates against schema (once added).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:17:30.311960939Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:07:13.842856580Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5","title":"[EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries","description":"# [EPIC] Export & reporting

## Mission
Make captured data portable and useful outside wa:
- export history slices
- share reproducible evidence for bugs
- generate summaries/reports

## Guiding principles
- Exports must be stable formats (JSONL/NDJSON) with version fields.
- Apply redaction by default.
- Prefer incremental exports over monolithic dumps.

## Definition of done
- `wa export` can export:
  - segments (with seq + timestamps)
  - gaps
  - events/detections
  - workflow executions + step logs
- Optional: export DB snapshot safely.


## Success Criteria
- Export paths (JSONL/NDJSON, DB snapshot, reports) are safe, redacted, and reproducible.
- Export artifacts include enough context to debug workflows/policy decisions without leaking secrets.
- Tests validate redaction and snapshot correctness; E2E validates end-to-end export flows.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Export format versioning and schema validation for each JSONL stream.
  - Redaction-by-default (and explicit opt-out behavior if we allow it).

- Integration tests:
  - Export from a fixture DB and assert stable ordering and completeness.

- E2E:
  - At least one E2E script must prove exports are included in artifact bundles and are secret-safe (`wa-4vx.10.18`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","title":"[Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa export`

## Goal
Export wa’s captured data into stable, composable formats.

## Export formats
- JSONL/NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
- Optional pretty JSON for small outputs

## Data to export
- output segments (including seq)
- output gaps
- events/detections (including rule_id/pack)
- workflow executions + step logs
- **pane reservations (active + historical)**

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--limit`

## Safety
- Apply redaction by default.
- Provide an explicit `--no-redact` that is hard to misuse (confirm / warn).

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable schemas/versioning
  - filtering correctness
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees
  - reservation export schema + redaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported JSONL can be re-ingested into analysis tools without additional context.

  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.3: Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown) ● P3

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.2","title":"DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks)","description":"# Task: DB snapshot export

## Goal
Allow exporting the wa SQLite database as a portable artifact for backup/debugging.

This supports:
- user self-service bug reports
- deterministic repro bundles
- safe backups for long-running watcher instances

## Safety requirements
- Must not corrupt or block the live DB.
- Must handle WAL mode correctly.
  - Prefer SQLite backup API (consistent snapshot) or a safe `VACUUM INTO` style approach.
  - If checkpointing is used, do it intentionally and document the tradeoffs.
- Must validate output size and warn if unexpectedly large.
- Must never include secrets beyond what already exists in the DB (and DB should already be redacted where required).

## Deliverables
- A human-facing entrypoint (name flexible):
  - `wa export db-snapshot --out <path>`
  - (or `wa export --db <path>`)
- Optional compression (feature-gated) for large snapshots.
- A short metadata sidecar (optional) that includes:
  - wa version
  - schema version
  - created_at
  - workspace id/path (redacted)

## Logging / UX
- Progress logs (bounded, non-spammy):
  - starting snapshot
  - checkpoint/backup method chosen
  - bytes written
  - completion path
- On failure: actionable error + suggestion to run `wa diag bundle`.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - output path validation (no directory traversal)
  - metadata rendering (if implemented)

- Integration tests:
  - create a temp DB with known rows
  - run snapshot export
  - open exported DB read-only and verify expected tables/rows exist
  - verify WAL handling does not leave a corrupt or partial snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported DB can be opened read-only and contains expected tables.
- Export is safe while watcher is running (no corruption; bounded blocking).
- Failures are actionable and do not leak secrets in logs.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.3","title":"Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown)","description":"# Task: Generate human-readable reports

## Goal
Turn raw history into actionable summaries for humans:
- what happened
- when
- what wa did
- what remains unhandled

## Reports (initial)
- Session report per pane (time-bounded):
  - major events
  - workflow runs + outcomes
  - gaps
- Compaction report:
  - compaction detected
  - context injection sent
  - verification result

## Output
- Markdown by default.
- JSON metadata sidecar optional.

## Testing
- Fixture-driven tests:
  - generate reports from a small fixture DB
  - snapshot/compare markdown output (stable headings + ordering)
  - assert redaction: no raw secrets or transcript content beyond allowed snippets

## Acceptance Criteria
- A report generated from a fixture DB is readable and self-contained.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.4","title":"Export tests + schema docs (versioned JSONL formats)","description":"# Task: Export tests + schema documentation

## Goal
Make exported formats trustworthy and long-lived.

## Deliverables
- Unit tests for:
  - correct filtering
  - correct redaction behavior
  - stable field naming
  - **reservation export schema + redaction**
- Documentation:
  - what each exported record type contains
  - versioning strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bd lint` / project lint passes and export tests cover at least one fixture end-to-end.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Validate exported JSONL against JSON Schemas in tests (not just snapshots).
  - Add a “schema drift” test that fails if an export field is removed/renamed without bumping the version.


LABELS: area-export, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2
